name: 2026-06-03 Political Commentary Senate Drama FDI Cost TH
description:
Sino ba talaga ang nagbabayad?

Net FDI fell to $1.03B in the first two months of 2026. Down from $1.58B in the same window last year.

A 35% slide while the Senate ran arrests, gunfire, and walkouts.

The political theater is not free. It is invoiced to the economy.

custom_fields.value:
Sino ba talaga ang nagbabayad?

Net FDI fell to $1.03B in the first two months of 2026. Down from $1.58B in the same window last year.

A 35% slide while the Senate ran arrests, gunfire, and walkouts.

The political theater is not free. It is invoiced to the economy.

Sino ba talaga ang nagbabayad?

Net FDI fell to $1.03B in the first two months of 2026. Down from $1.58B in the same window last year.

A 35% slide while the Senate ran arrests, gunfire, and walkouts.

The political theater is not free. It is invoiced to the economy.

Malacanang on 2026-06-02 publicly warned that ongoing Senate turmoil (May 11-13 events: arrest attempt on Sen. dela Rosa, in-chamber gunfire 2026-05-13, majority bloc walkout) is impeding passage of priority bills and weighing on investor confidence ahead of Congress's mid-year break starting 2026-06-06. BSP FDI data (released 2026-05-11) shows net FDI Jan-Feb 2026 at $1.03B vs $1.58B same period 2025, a 35% decline; net equity placements down 12.5% to $171M; intercompany borrowings down 38.8% to $734M.
